title: Tile keyboard navigation does not trap focus or handle Escape
MarketTickerStrip does not handle null/stale quote data gracefully
QuickSetup API key validation allows whitespace-only and case-sensitive symbols
Recent tickers row does not announce empty state or overflow behavior
CommandPalette does not persist open state or handle modal backdrop clicks
Launchpad hero overflow and responsive scaling issues on mobile
body:
## Problem LaunchpadHero (src/renderer/src/shell/LaunchpadHome.tsx:400-463) displays a large serif heading (48px) and greeting message, but does not adapt to narrow viewports or high zoom levels. On a mobile-sized Electron window (<480px), the greeting text overflows horizontally, and the hero font sizes do not scale down. Additionally, the hero is not subject to the max-w-5xl container constraint properly, so it can bleed into the edges on ultrawide displays. ## Evidence - `src/renderer/src/shell/LaunchpadHome.tsx:441-447` — hero heading with fixed `text-[48px]`, no responsive variant - Line 442: date with uppercase `text-[12px]` fixed size - Line 445: greeting with `text-[48px]` — no `md:text-[36px]` or `sm:text-[28px]` breakpoints - Line 448: market status with fixed `text-[13px]`, no scaling - Container constraint at line 292 (`max-w-5xl`) applies to wrapper but hero is full width inside ## Expected - Hero heading should scale down on small screens: `text-[28px] md:text-[36px] lg:text-[48px]` - Date and status text should scale proportionally - Hero should not overflow on narrower viewports - Test at 375px (mobile), 768px (tablet), 1920px (ultrawide) ## Proposed fix ```jsx function LaunchpadHero(): React.JSX.Element { // ... existing code ... return (
{date}
{greeting}.
{/* ... */}
) } ``` ## Impact Users on smaller Electron windows (common for floating window apps) experience poor UX with text overflow and poor visual hierarchy. Medium-priority polish issue.

title: Shell route guard 'isFullbleed' does not cover all Launchpad-style routes
body:
## Problem

Shell.tsx (line 18-19) defines `isFullbleed` as: `location.pathname === '/' || location.pathname === '' || location.pathname === '/quick-setup'`. This condition hides TopBar, ModuleSwitcher, and StatusBar for Launchpad and QuickSetup. However, if new full-bleed routes are added (e.g., onboarding flow, tutorial, login), they must be manually added to this list or they will render with unnecessary chrome. This is error-prone and easy to miss.

## Evidence

- `src/renderer/src/shell/Shell.tsx:18-19` — hardcoded route list
- Line 41-50: conditional rendering based on isFullbleed
- router.tsx (line 69-106): routes are defined separately, no metadata about which are fullbleed
- If a new fullbleed route is added to router, Shell must be updated separately

## Expected

- Route metadata should indicate if a route is fullbleed (option: add `fullbleed: true` to router config)
- OR: isFullbleed should check against a constant route list defined in one place
- OR: Module/page components should be able to signal fullbleed requirement via context or hook

## Proposed fix

Option A (simplest): Create a constant list of fullbleed routes:
```jsx
// src/renderer/src/lib/constants.ts
export const FULLBLEED_ROUTES = ['/', '', '/quick-setup']

// src/renderer/src/shell/Shell.tsx
const isFullbleed = FULLBLEED_ROUTES.includes(location.pathname)
```

Option B (router metadata): Add fullbleed flag to route definitions and use a hook to check current route.

## Impact

New routes may accidentally render with TopBar/Sidebar, breaking design intent. Low-priority but affects future development velocity. Quick fix: 5 mins.

title: RecentTickersStore case normalization is inconsistent with market data APIs
TopBar command palette button not accessible via keyboard without Cmd/Ctrl
body: ## Problem TopBar (src/renderer/src/shell/TopBar.tsx:46-59) includes a button to toggle the command palette, with a hint showing "⌘K" (Cmd+K). However, the button itself is not always keyboard accessible in the traditional sense: it uses an onClick handler and is reachable via Tab, but screen readers may not announce the keyboard shortcut hint. Additionally, the button text "Search tickers, actions…" does not clearly indicate it opens a palette or that it's interactive; it reads more like a placeholder. ## Evidence - `src/renderer/src/shell/TopBar.tsx:46-59` — button with onClick and title attribute - Line 48-51: className includes `transition-colors hover:text-[var(--color-fg)]` but no focus ring visible in the code (relies on browser default or global CSS) - Line 55: span with placeholder text - Line 56-58: kbd element shows shortcut hint, good for sighted users but screen readers may skip it ## Expected - Button should have aria-label clearly indicating it opens command palette - Keyboard shortcut hint should be announced to screen readers (aria-keyshortcuts) - Button should have visible focus ring (not relied on browser default) - Consider adding keyboard shortcut to title or aria-label ## Proposed fix ```jsx ``` ## Impact Screen reader users don't learn about the Cmd+K shortcut. Medium-priority because alternative ways to open palette exist (via Launchpad), but improves discoverability.
